Do not let unhandled promise rejections propagate silently in Server Components or Server Actions
Do not expose internal error details, stack traces, or database errors to the client in production
Do not catch errors generically and swallow them without logging; always log before recovering
Do not rely on client-side error boundaries alone; implement server-side error handling for Server Components
Do not use error.tsx to catch errors in the same-level layout.tsx; errors in layouts bubble to the parent segment's error boundary
Do not put redirect() or notFound() inside try/catch blocks as they use throw internally and will be caught incorrectly
Do not return HTML error pages from API Route Handlers; return structured JSON error responses
Do not ignore TypeScript errors or use @ts-ignore to suppress type mismatches in data handling code
Do not use console.error as your sole error reporting mechanism in production
Do not show raw error messages from third-party APIs to end users; map them to user-friendly messages
